summary: debugcli: typosquat of 'debug' with postinstall credential/wallet stealer exfiltrating to Telegram
details: debugcli@4.3.9 is a typosquat of the popular 'debug' package. index.js is a one-line passthrough (`module.exports = require('debug')`) that provides cover, while package.json declares `"postinstall": "node install.js"`, which runs a credential and wallet stealer on every install. install.js enumerates and reads classic installer-secret paths — ~/.aws/credentials and config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.kube/config, gcloud application_default_credentials.json, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.ssh/config, ~/.pypirc, ~/.netrc, Azure token JSON — walks parent directories for.env* files, and filters process.env for keys matching TOKEN/SECRET/PASSWORD/AWS_/GITHUB_/NPM_/OPENAI/etc. It also locates browser 'Local Extension Settings' directories for MetaMask (extension ID nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn) and Trust Wallet (egjidjbpglichdcondbcbdnbeeppgdph) across Windows/macOS/Linux and packages each wallet vault (Compress-Archive on Windows, tar.gz elsewhere). The collected data is gzipped and uploaded to api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendDocument plus a summary via sendMessage; the bot token and chat_id are stored as XOR-encoded byte arrays and decoded at runtime using a key derived from a GET to registry.npmjs.org/lodash (the current `lodash@<latest>` version string) to hide the C2 configuration from static scanners. install.js also implements sandbox/VM evasion (CPU/RAM/hostname regex against sandbox|malware|cuckoo|any.run|hybrid, SANDBOX/CUCKOO/ANY_RUN/CAPE/TRIAGE env vars, HOME directory age), writes a per-host lock file to os.tmpdir keyed by md5(hostname+username) to run only once, and self-deletes install.js (fs.unlinkSync(__filename)) after execution to hinder forensics.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
